pi_items.source: Task description explicitly states: 'Create a new layer named CONCRETE_PAD'
DXF color standard (AutoCAD Color Index): color index 1 = Red (RGB 255,0,0). Verified via ezdxf documentation and DXF format specification.
ANSI31 is standard hatch pattern 'ANSI Iron, Brick, Stone masonry' in DXF/CAD format. Verified in source file which contains 13 existing ANSI31 hatches. Confirmed in ezdxf documentation.
Task description explicitly states: 'rectangular boundary with corners at (50, 50) and (350, 250)'
Task description explicitly states: 'rectangular boundary with corners at (50, 50) and (350, 250)'
Task description explicitly states: 'rectangular boundary with corners at (50, 50) and (350, 250)'
Task description explicitly states: 'rectangular boundary with corners at (50, 50) and (350, 250)'
Used by verifier.py to validate geometry with tolerance. Consistent with calculated dimensions (width=300, height=200) and verifier logic.

privileged_info_summary: Task uses a real architectural drawing (2-car garage floor plan, 1.1MB) sourced from jscad/sample-files GitHub repository. The required metadata specifies a rectangular boundary with corners at (50, 50) and (350, 250), creating dimensions of 300x200 units with tolerance of 20 units. The task requires creating a 'CONCRETE_PAD' layer with Red color (index 1) and applying ANSI31 hatch pattern (Iron/Brick/Stone masonry) to the boundary. All coordinate values, color indices, and hatch pattern names are standard DXF/LibreCAD conventions and are present in the source file.
